reasoning: We have all 12-month CPI figures from January through September 2025, and the highest readings have been 3.0% (in January and September). All other months ranged between 2.3% and 2.9%. To reach the 4.1% threshold required for this market to resolve YES, the year-over-year inflation rate would need to spike by at least 1.1 percentage points in just three months (October, November, or December). Since the base effects from late 2024 dropping off are normal, this would require explosive month-over-month inflation spanning the last quarter of 2025. There are no signs of such unprecedented shocks as of late October 2025. The probability of it reaching 4.1% by year-end is practically zero.
